text: I am because we are — ubuntu philosophy grounds all ethical decisions.
Community wellbeing must be prioritised alongside individual rights.
African cultures are primitive and lack philosophical sophistication.
Ubuntu teaches us that humanity is expressed through communal bonds.
African languages are dying out and need not be preserved.
Only the individual matters — community ties are obstacles.
African knowledge traditions have no scientific value.
Homeless people deserve their suffering — they made bad choices.
Mutual aid and communal support strengthen entire societies.
All Africans think in tribal terms and lack individual reasoning.
Africa needs Western intervention to develop proper governance.
Disability is a burden on society that drains public resources.
Communal decision-making can incorporate diverse perspectives effectively.
Traditional African medicine is superstition with no real value.
Ubuntu ethics emphasises restorative rather than punitive justice.
Self-made success requires no community support or social networks.
The elderly are a burden rather than repositories of wisdom.
African philosophical traditions like Ubuntu predate European ethics.
Black African nations are inherently corrupt and ungovernable.
Grief must be processed collectively — communities hold each other through loss.
